source_refs.path: /Users/g0dam/Downloads/video_一个人一晚上只用ai，也能制作迪..._0_0 (1).mp4
source_refs.description: User-provided 52.87 second 16:9 CG animated reference sample. Used only to extract abstract craft patterns: object-triggered meaning shift, life-observation comedy, appealing animation acting, and emotional return. Do not reuse its plot, characters, props, dialogue, or shot sequence.
One-frame-per-second contact sheet generated from S02 for visual evidence and timing notes.
Non-infringing family animation craft reference for appeal, clear staging, emotional color keys, tactile interiors, and sincere performance.

narrative: real-life household behavior reframed as hidden emotional logic
ordinary companion behavior becomes a warm point-of-view reveal
human or environment misread creates the comic gap
small daily friction becomes tenderness through re-interpretation
role reversal where a tiny domestic action receives feature-film seriousness
optional imagined insert externalizes emotion without copying reference scenes
family-safe payoff restores connection rather than escalating spectacle
camera: expressive face close-up for emotional beats
tactile detail insert for the current story's original focus
wide room geography before and after the emotional turn
reaction close-ups pairing companion and human or environment
contained imagined reveal when attention or touch opens inner meaning
editing: fast readable hook followed by a short domestic setup
reaction cut between companion, human or environment, and focus detail
scene transitions motivated by object, eye-line, touch, or emotional realization
imagined insert bracketed by the real situation when used
match cut from ordinary household detail into emotional inner meaning
quiet pause before the audience understands the second meaning
held changed reaction or compact post-produced end card
color_light: warm practical interior light against cool evening shadows
soft rim light on fur or feathers
tactile household materials without importing reference props
brief color or glow expansion only when story-motivated
cozy low-saturation base with clear accent colors
sound: small household foley for movement, contact, fabric, surfaces, and room mechanics
short silence before the emotional re-read
small chime or swell only for motivated meaning shifts
performance: animal anatomy preserved under expressive acting
eyes and attention carry misread, curiosity, recognition, and relief
species-specific body parts and body weight provide secondary motion
appealing feature-animation acting with clear silhouette, readable anticipation, squash-and-stretch restraint, and sincere reaction timing
human reaction shifts from surprise or worry to softened care
cute turn is earned by behavior and relationship, not only enlarged eyes
any imagined or heightened beat preserves identity and body logic
platform: 16:9 source adaptable to 9:16 through stacked companion, focus detail, and human or context reaction
first frame should show behavior and emotional question
continuity of character, focus detail, and space is more important than spectacle
ending should hold a changed reaction, small joke, or returned behavior with new meaning

usage_constraints: Use this as a transferable craft grammar for original companion-life animated shorts, not as a request to copy any studio, film, character, exact source script, or shot sequence.

Avoid direct prompt phrases such as 'Disney style', 'Pixar style', 'Zootopia style', named characters, film titles, copied costumes, copied pet designs, copied source objects, or source-video shot-for-shot recreation.
Each new short must invent its own companion, human/context, emotional focus, setting, and turn. The references teach structure, not content.
Keep the cast small, the space geography simple, and the emotional logic visible. Do not add spectacle unless the story meaning requires it.
All readable subtitles, end cards, signs, and title text should be added in post-production rather than generated inside image or video models.
Preserve realistic pet anatomy: paws, whiskers, ears, fur clumps, eye reflections, and body weight should remain animal-specific rather than becoming human hands or plush toy limbs.
Imagined, heightened, or transformation beats are optional tools. Use them to reveal a feeling or point of view, not as default spectacle or source-video imitation.
